PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Apr-02-2008 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES- ATTY. ELLIOT PISOR FOR THE RESPONDENT YOLANDA PAUL;
ATTY. RICHARD CANATELLA FOR HENRY STEVENS;
ATTY. JONATHAN FERDON, FOR THE PUBLIC ADMINISTRATOR, SPECIAL ADMINISTRATOR.

MR. CANATELLA REPORTED THAT HE FILED AN APPEAL REGARDING JUDGE MELLON'S DECISION REGARDING THE DEED OF THE REAL PROPERTY BEING TRANSFERRED TO THE DAUGHTER. THERE IS NO PROPERTY AND NO NEED TO ADMIT FOR PROBATE. MR. PISOR REPORTED THAT THERE IS A VALID DEED AND YOLANDA PAUL NOW OWNS THE HOUSE. HIS MAIN CONCERN IS ABOUT MR. STEVEN'S INITIATING 3 ACTIONS AND THAT HE HAS STANDING TO BE APPOINTED PERSONAL REPRESENTATIVE. HE WAS PAID $70,000 IN ATTORNEY'S FEE AND WANTS THE WILL TO BE ADMITTED IN PROBATE. MR. FERDON REPORTED THAT HE HAD ACCOMPLISHED WHAT THE COURT WANTS HIM TO DO. THERE IS NO FURTHER ACTION NEEDED, THE COURT TOOK THE MATTER OFF-CALENDAR UNTIL THE RULING BY THE UPPER COURT. NOTICE WILL BE GIVEN TO MR. FERDON OF THE APPELLATE COURT'S RULING BY MR. CANATELLA. Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Leticia Perez, CSR# 7782. Clerk: Cynthia Herbert/ S. Ravinantapricha. Bailiff: J. Garrido.

In addition, he requested that case be kept open pending the appeal and stated that there was no necessity to admit the will as there was no estate. The Court stated that Mr. Macleod was not in court today. The matter was continued to April 2, 2008.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon (Counsel for the San Francisco Public Administrator, Special Administrator); Ronald Toran (Counsel for Henry Stevens, Petitioner); Norman Mcleod (Counsel for Yolanda Paul, Petitioner).

Mr. Ferdon advised the Court of a number of administrtive issues, including access to the real property, status of the real property (taxes, mortgage, if occupied) and the contents of the residence and the safe. Counsel discussed the various issues raised by Mr. Ferdon. The Court ordered the parties to cooperate with the Public Administration in the administration of the premises and continued the matter to June 19, 2007 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204 at which time a trial date will be set.

Pro bono mediation was declined at this time.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Feb-13-2007 Continued To Mar-27-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Canatella (Counsel for Henry Stevens, Objector); Norman Mcleod (Counsel for Yolanda Paul, Petitioner); Jonathan Ferdon (Counsel for the San Francisco Public Administrator ("SFPA")).

Procedural Note: Counsel appeared at the calendar call but after presentation to the Court determined that certain issues were not as yet resolved and recessed to the Court's conference room for further discussion. Counsel returned on record at 9:30 a.m.

After extensive discussion, Mr. Ferdon represented to the Court that all counsel had stipulated to the appointment of the San Francisco Public Administrator as Special Administrator for this estate, for limited purposes only, to serve until such time as the right to possession of the real property is determined. Additional various responsibilities will be handled by Mr. Canatella and his client (i.e., the collection of information with respect to SSI distributions and tax returns) and also by Mr. Mcleod (i.e., the determination of the status of the real property). Mr. Canatella's client will bear the expense of the Public Administrator's fees. All matters were then continued to March 27, 2007 for status by which time Mr. Canatella and/or his client must file any responsive pleadings or objections. Mr. Ferdon is to prepare the Order appointing SFPA the Special Administrator.

Judicial Office: Stella Pantazis, Judge Pro Tempore; Reporter: David Davenport CSR #1694; Bailiff: Deputy Jimmy Peralta; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Court Examiner: Christine Nahnson.

Off Calendar Nov-21-2006 Continued To Jan-09-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Richard Canatella appearing for petitioner Henry Stevens on the petition for letters of administration. Ronald Toran and Richard Canatella appearing for Henry Stevens on the 850 petition. Norman Macleod appearing for and with respondent/objector Yolanda Paul. Mr. Macleod stated that he has not file the Will for Probate because there are no assets in the estate. Court cannot make the finding of intestacy because there is a Will. Counsel will need to open probate so all the issues can come before the Court. Mr. Canatella asked the Court to appoint his client as the Special Administrator. Mr. Macleod objected and he wants his client to be appointed as the Special Administrator. Mr. Canatella objected. Court thought it may be best to appoint an independent party. There is no petition to appoint special administrator before the Court at this time. Counsel can file their petition for appointment of special administrator and set it for hearing on the ex parte calendar. Both petitions are continued to 1/9/07. Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Floyd Butler, CSR#2518. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Mizota.
